notes: Spec asserts: state stays working through the queued-message arrival, no →ready flicker when the queued input lands, the cycle still ends at ready. The recipe asks kiro to count slowly (so the working span is long enough to type into); the driver then sends a second prompt mid-turn via a single tmux send-keys send (no preceding wait_turn) which kiro-cli's V2 TUI queues until the next safe point. The queued text lives inside the kiro process — it is NOT appended to the <uuid>.jsonl transcript until consumed — so the parser sees one contiguous working span (AssistantMessage(toolUse)/ToolResults lines keep it working; only a text-only AssistantMessage is turn_done). The queued_window phase pins the no-flicker invariant with duration_at_least_ms 3000. The recording does NOT assert whether the queued message is folded into the current turn or consumed as a new turn — both are legal per spec. kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost (sidecar only), so observations assert model unknown, cost/tokens zero.

text: First user prompt drives ready → working

A second user prompt is typed into the TUI mid-turn and queued by kiro's V2 TUI. State must stay working — no flicker to ready when the queued input lands, no flicker to waiting (spec bullets 1 and 2)
The cycle eventually ends at ready (spec bullet 3) — either the queued message is folded into the current turn or consumed as a follow-up turn; both shapes finish in ready
